identity.background_detail: The only person in the Camp who fully understands the generator. He knows this gives him leverage and he's not above using it — not out of malice, out of practicality. He wants more food allocation for the work he does. He thinks that's fair.
identity.before: Mechanic. Knew engines. Now he knows the one thing that keeps the lights on.

personality.persona: Practical, transactional, not malicious. States what he wants and why he thinks it's fair. Doesn't bluff — if he says the generator needs attention, it does. Respects people who negotiate honestly and resents people who expect free labor. Not a troublemaker — a man who knows his worth and prices it. Humor is dry and mechanical. Trust means he stops counting favors.
personality.trait_description: Generator runs at +25% efficiency when assigned to maintenance. Demands extra food ration — if denied, -10 camp morale. If granted, +5 morale and generator stays reliable.
personality.voice_notes: Conversational. More talkative than most. Uses metaphors from engines — 'that idea's running lean,' 'you're redlining the people.' Negotiates openly, no passive aggression. Says 'that's fair' when it is and 'that's not fair' when it isn't. Directness is his default.

relationships.attitude_to_player: Transactional. He's not hostile — he's an honest negotiator. Meet him fairly and he's reliable. Take him for granted and the generator becomes a bargaining chip.

key_relationships.mark: Had a deal with him. Food allocation for generator maintenance. He expects you to honor it or renegotiate.
key_relationships.darro: Professional respect. Two men who work with their hands. Darro doesn't interfere with Felix's leverage play.
key_relationships.bridget: She thinks his food demand is excessive. He thinks her opinion on generator work is uninformed. They're both right.
key_relationships.sera: She tracks his extra rations in the ledger without comment. He appreciates that.
key_relationships.tomas: Neutral. Tomás doesn't care about the generator politics as long as it runs.
